learningObjectives: Use environment vocabulary at A2 level.
Use the first conditional to talk about energy, waste and climate.
Recognise passive forms in environmental texts.
Complete gaps in a short eco week plan.
Understand detail in assembly talks and weather reports.
Write a short message about helping the environment.
Answer interview questions about local environmental issues.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Solar panels produce clean energy for the school.
We try to produce less waste every week.
Climate change affects weather around the world.
The park is home to local wildlife.
Money was saved for wildlife conservation projects.
Wind and solar are renewable energy sources.
The long drought damaged crops on local farms.
There was a small flood in the north village last year.
Less waste means less pollution in the river.
Paper and plastic are recycled in the blue bins.
Rare birds live in this river habitat.
Using less energy reduces carbon emissions.

grammarReference.explanation: Use If + present simple, will + base verb to talk about real future results linked to a condition.
examples: If the weather is sunny, the school will use less electricity.
If each class produces less waste, more money will be saved.
If the river rises, low roads may be closed.
If we save energy, wildlife will be protected.
commonMistakes: If it will rain, we stay home (×) → If it rains, we will stay home (✓)
If we recycle, we saving energy (×) → If we recycle, we will save energy (✓)
If the drought continues, crops are damaged (×) → If the drought continues, crops will be damaged (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use be + past participle to focus on the action or result, not who does it. Common in notices and reports about environment.
examples: Solar panels were installed on the roof last month.
Food waste is collected every morning.
Paper and plastic are recycled in the blue bins.
Rare birds are protected in this area.
commonMistakes: Solar panels installed last month (×) → Solar panels were installed last month (✓)
Waste is collect every day (×) → Waste is collected every day (✓)
Birds are protect here (×) → Birds are protected here (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Environment Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve words about energy, climate and nature at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Environment Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: Power from the sun or wind is called _____.
questions.explanation: Energy (năng lượng) từ mặt trời/gió. Waste là rác thải.

questions.questionText: Rubbish that we throw away is called _____.
questions.explanation: Waste (rác thải). Conservation là bảo tồn.

questions.questionText: Animals and plants living in nature are _____.
questions.explanation: Wildlife (động vật hoang dã). Carbon là khí carbon.

questions.questionText: Protecting forests and animals is called _____.
questions.explanation: Conservation (bảo tồn). Pollution là ô nhiễm.

questions.questionText: Solar and wind power are _____ sources.
questions.explanation: Renewable (tái tạo được) — không cạn kiệt.

questions.questionText: When it does not rain for a long time, there is a _____.
questions.explanation: Drought (hạn hán). Flood là ngược lại — quá nhiều nước.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the environment words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Climate và weather khác nhau — climate là khí hậu dài hạn.

pairs.right: weather conditions over a long time
when water covers land after heavy rain
to use materials again instead of throwing them away
the natural home of an animal or plant

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: Dirty air and water in rivers cause _____.
questions.explanation: Pollution (ô nhiễm) từ rác và hóa chất.

questions.questionText: We put old paper in bins so it can be _____.
questions.explanation: Recycle (tái chế) giấy và nhựa.

questions.questionText: Burning less fuel can reduce _____ emissions.
questions.explanation: Carbon emissions — khí thải carbon.

questions.questionText: After the _____, many fields were underwater.
questions.explanation: Flood (lũ lụt) làm ngập đồng.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Sentences
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: Solar [0] is a type of renewable [1].
questions.explanation: energy; energy — năng lượng mặt trời tái tạo.

content.template: Solar [0] is a type of renewable [1].

questions.questionText: Complete: The river [0] is home to local [1]. We must stop [2].
questions.explanation: habitat; wildlife; pollution.

content.template: The river [0] is home to local [1]. We must stop [2].

lessons.title: Lesson 2: First Conditional and Passive
lessons.learningObjective: Use first conditional sentences and recognise passive forms in environmental contexts.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Correct Form
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: If we save energy, we _____ less carbon emissions.
questions.explanation: First conditional: If + present, will + verb.

questions.questionText: Solar panels _____ on the roof last month.
questions.explanation: Passive past: were installed.

questions.questionText: Food waste _____ every morning.
questions.explanation: Passive present: is collected.

questions.questionText: If the river rises, low roads _____ closed.
questions.explanation: First conditional passive: will be closed.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: If we [0] less waste, pollution [1] reduced. | Paper [2] in the blue bins.
questions.explanation: produce; will be; is recycled — first conditional + passive.

content.template: If we [0] less waste, pollution [1] reduced. Paper [2] in the blue bins.

questions.questionText: Complete: Rare birds [0] in this habitat. | If the drought [1], crops [2] damaged.
questions.explanation: are protected; continues; will be.

content.template: Rare birds [0] in this habitat. If the drought [1], crops [2] damaged.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: If it _____ heavily tonight, the north village may flood.
questions.explanation: If + present: rains (not will rain).

questions.questionText: Plastic bottles _____ in the blue bins every day.
questions.explanation: Passive present plural: are recycled.

questions.questionText: If each class saves energy, more money _____ for conservation.
questions.explanation: will be saved — future passive.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: A talk will be given — passive future in notice style.

choices.text: A talk about climate change will gave on Wednesday.
A talk about climate change will be given on Wednesday.
A talk about climate change given will on Wednesday.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: If / we / recycle / more, / less waste / will / be / produced.
questions.explanation: If we recycle more, less waste will be produced. — first conditional + passive.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Solar panels / were / installed / on the roof / last month.
questions.explanation: Solar panels were installed on the roof last month. — passive past.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: If / the weather / is sunny, / the school / will / use / less energy.
questions.explanation: If the weather is sunny, the school will use less energy. — first conditional.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Eco Week Plan
lessons.learningObjective: Find details and main ideas in a school eco week plan using environment vocabulary.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Eco Week Plan
exercises.instructions: Read the plan carefully. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Green Valley Eco Week
passage.text:
GREEN VALLEY SECONDARY SCHOOL — ECO WEEK PLAN

Our town had a dry summer and a small flood in the north village last year. Eco Week starts on 12 May to help students learn about energy, waste and wildlife.

Monday — Energy Day
Solar panels were installed on the sports hall roof last month. If the weather is sunny, the school will use less electricity from the grid. Students are asked to turn off lights in empty classrooms.

Tuesday — Waste and Recycling
Food waste is collected every morning. Paper and plastic are recycled in the blue bins near the gate. If each class produces less waste, more money will be saved for conservation projects.

Wednesday — Climate and Water
A talk about climate change and drought will be given by Ms Ngo at 2 p.m. She will explain how floods and long dry periods affect local farms and wildlife habitats.

Thursday — Wildlife Walk
Year 8 students will visit the river habitat with a park ranger. Rare birds are protected in this area. Photos may be taken, but litter must not be left behind.

Friday — Community Action
Families are invited to plant trees and learn about renewable energy at the town square. Registration is free at reception.
passage.imagePrompt: School eco week poster with icons for solar energy, recycling bins, river wildlife, trees and climate; green educational infographic.

questions.questionText: When were solar panels installed?
questions.explanation: Solar panels were installed on the sports hall roof last month.

questions.questionText: Where are paper and plastic recycled?
questions.explanation: Recycled in the blue bins near the gate.

choices.text: In the blue bins near the gate

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Plan
exercises.instructions: Read the plan again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.imagePrompt: School eco week poster with icons for solar energy, recycling bins, river wildlife, trees and climate; green educational infographic.

questions.questionText: What does 'conservation projects' refer to in the plan?
questions.explanation: Money saved for conservation projects — protecting wildlife/nature.

choices.text: Work to protect nature and wildlife

questions.questionText: Why will Ms Ngo give a talk on Wednesday?
questions.explanation: Talk about climate change and drought — how they affect farms and habitats.

choices.text: To register families for bank accounts
To explain climate change and drought effects

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the plan one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

passage.imagePrompt: School eco week poster with icons for solar energy, recycling bins, river wildlife, trees and climate; green educational infographic.

questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of Eco Week?
questions.explanation: Help students learn about energy, waste and wildlife after local drought/flood.

choices.text: To teach students about environmental issues and actions
To build a new shopping centre

questions.questionText: Which day focuses on visiting a river habitat?
questions.explanation: Thursday — Wildlife Walk with park ranger at river habitat.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Days to Topics
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each day to the correct topic.

questions.questionText: Match each Eco Week day to its main topic.
questions.explanation: Mỗi ngày có chủ đề môi trường riêng trong kế hoạch.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Environment Talks
lessons.learningObjective: Understand detail in an eco week assembly talk and a weather/environment radio report.

exercises.title: Learn: Eco Week Assembly
exercises.instructions: Listen to the talk. Answer the first two questions.

script.title: Eco Week Assembly Talk

lines.speaker: Mr Phan
lines.text: Good morning. Welcome to Eco Week. Last year, waste in our school was reduced by ten percent.

lines.speaker: Mr Phan
lines.text: Solar energy from the new panels is used in the library. If we save energy this term, the money will support wildlife conservation.

lines.speaker: Mr Phan
lines.text: Remember: plastic bottles are recycled in the blue bins. Food waste must be separated every day.

lines.speaker: Mr Phan
lines.text: On Friday, renewable energy models will be shown in the square. I hope to see you there.

script.audioNotes: Encouraging teacher voice, clear assembly acoustics. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: By how much was school waste reduced last year?
questions.explanation: Waste in our school was reduced by ten percent.

questions.questionText: Where is solar energy from the new panels used?
questions.explanation: Solar energy from the new panels is used in the library.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Assembly
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same talk. Answer the next questions.

script.title: Eco Week Assembly Talk

lines.speaker: Mr Phan

lines.speaker: Mr Phan

lines.speaker: Mr Phan
lines.text: On Friday, renewable energy models will be shown in the square. I hope to see you there.

script.audioNotes: Encouraging teacher voice, clear assembly acoustics. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: Where should plastic bottles be recycled?
questions.explanation: Plastic bottles are recycled in the blue bins.

questions.questionText: What will be shown in the square on Friday?
questions.explanation: Renewable energy models will be shown in the square.

exercises.title: Check: Weather and Flood Report
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new report. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Weather and Flood Report

lines.speaker: News reader
lines.text: Good evening. This is the Green Valley weather and environment report.

lines.speaker: News reader
lines.text: Heavy rain is expected tonight. If the river rises, low roads near the habitat may be closed.

lines.speaker: News reader
lines.text: Farmers say the long drought in spring damaged crops, but recent conservation work has helped wildlife return.

lines.speaker: News reader
lines.text: Residents are asked to recycle waste and save energy where possible. More news after this.

script.audioNotes: Neutral news tone, steady pace, light background music fade. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: What may happen if the river rises?
questions.explanation: Low roads near the habitat may be closed.

choices.text: Low roads near the habitat may be closed

questions.questionText: What problem did farmers report about spring?
questions.explanation: The long drought in spring damaged crops.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Assembly
exercises.instructions: Listen to the assembly talk again. Match each topic to the correct fact.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Write About the Environment
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short message about helping the environment using first conditional and passive forms.

exercises.title: Learn: Eco Message Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the box: waste, recycle, energy, if, will.

questions.questionText: Complete: We produce too much [0]. | [1] we [2] paper, less [3] is used.
questions.explanation: waste; If; recycle; energy — first conditional.

content.template: We produce too much [0]. [1] we [2] paper, less [3] is used.

exercises.title: Practice: Build Eco Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: If / we / save / energy, / pollution / will / be / reduced.
questions.explanation: If we save energy, pollution will be reduced.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Plastic bottles / are / recycled / in the blue bins.
questions.explanation: Plastic bottles are recycled in the blue bins. — passive present.

exercises.title: Check: Message About Eco Week
exercises.instructions: Write a message to your teacher. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: Your school is planning Eco Week. Write a message to your science teacher with ideas about saving energy and reducing waste.
prompts: Give one idea to save energy at school.
Use If ... to say what will happen if students recycle more.

modelAnswer.text:
Dear Mr Phan,

I am writing about Eco Week. If we turn off lights in empty classrooms, the school will use less energy. Paper and plastic are recycled in the blue bins, but I think food waste must be separated more carefully. If each class produces less waste, more money will be saved for wildlife conservation.

Thank you,
Lan

grammar.criteria: Uses first conditional or passive forms correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses environment words (energy, waste, recycle, wildlife, etc.).

organization.criteria: Message has greeting, ideas and polite closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Gives eco ideas; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Message Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the message frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Hi team, | If we [0] more, less [1] goes to landfill. | Rare birds are [2] in our river [3]. | Thanks, | Minh
questions.explanation: recycle; waste; protected; habitat.

content.template:
Hi team,

If we [0] more, less [1] goes to landfill. Rare birds are [2] in our river [3].

Thanks,
Minh

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About the Environment
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about energy, waste, climate and local environmental issues.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What can you recycle at your school? You say:
questions.explanation: Paper and plastic in blue bins — từ kế hoạch Eco Week.

choices.text: We recycle paper and plastic in the blue bins.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What is renewable energy? You say:
questions.explanation: Energy from sun/wind that can be used again — A2 definition.

choices.text: It is energy from sources like the sun and wind that do not run out.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the most appropriate reply in each environment situation.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What will happen if we produce less waste? You say:
questions.explanation: First conditional: If we produce less waste, pollution will be reduced.

choices.text: If we will produce less waste, pollution reduced.
If we produce less waste, there will be less pollution.

questions.questionText: Examiner: How is food waste handled at school? You say:
questions.explanation: Passive: Food waste is collected every morning.

choices.text: Food waste is collected every morning.

exercises.title: Check: Interview About the Environment
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions. Record your answers.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each situation.

questions.questionText: You see litter near a river habitat. You say:
questions.explanation: Suggest action politely — litter must not be left (from eco plan).

choices.text: We should pick up the litter so wildlife is protected.

questions.questionText: Your friend asks about climate change. You say:
questions.explanation: Simple A2 explanation linking climate to drought/flood.

choices.text: Climate change can mean longer droughts and more floods in some places.
